title: sedum rosea subsp. frigidum
intro: Sedum rosea subsp. frigidum, also known as the Wood's Spotted Stonecrop or Frigid Sedum, is a species of succulent plant in the family Crassulaceae. This plant is primarily used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, derived from its sap and leaves. The health benefits of Sedum rosea subsp. frigidum include relief from fever, sore throats, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Sedum rosea subsp. frigidum is traditionally prepared as a tea or tincture, or applied topically in the form of a salve or ointment, to harness its medicinal properties.
